name: Brownfield
description_short: Brownfield Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution in Brownfield, TX offering savings accounts, share certificates, and online banking with a $25 minimum deposit.
description_long:
Brownfield Federal Credit Union is a member-owned and controlled financial services organization serving the Brownfield, Texas community. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates under the mission of meeting members' financial needs through quality service at competitive rates while maintaining financial stability. The institution emphasizes building lifelong relationships with its membership base, positioning itself as a local alternative to traditional banks.

The credit union offers savings accounts (share accounts) with a low $25 minimum deposit requirement that is returned if the account is closed. Members earn interest on their savings, with dividends posted quarterly. The institution also provides online banking access, allowing members to manage their accounts remotely. Share certificates are available as additional savings products, though specific terms are not detailed on the website content provided.

Brownfield FCU distinguishes itself through its member-owned structure, which means profits are returned to members rather than external shareholders. The $25 minimum deposit is notably accessible compared to many financial institutions, making it easy for members to establish accounts. As an NCUA-insured credit union, member deposits are federally protected up to $250,000.

A significant consideration is that the savings rate has recently declined substantially—the APR was reduced from 0.50% to 0.25% effective April 1, 2026, representing a 50% rate cut. This places the institution's savings offerings at or below rates offered by many online banks and larger credit unions. The website does not provide comprehensive information about other potential services such as personal loans, credit cards, or mortgages, limiting visibility into the full scope of offerings.
services: Member savings accounts (share accounts) with quarterly dividend payments
Account opening with low $25 minimum deposit
Local branch banking in Brownfield, TX
NCUA deposit insurance protection up to $250,000
pros: NCUA-insured member deposits with up to $250,000 federal protection
Low $25 minimum deposit to open savings accounts—not a fee, deposit is refunded if account closes
Member-owned structure means profits return to members, not external shareholders
Quarterly dividend payments on savings accounts
Online banking access for account management
Local community focus with established presence in Brownfield, TX
Interest-bearing savings account even on minimal deposits
cons: Savings rate recently cut 50% from 0.50% APR to 0.25% APR (effective April 1, 2026)
Current 0.25% APR is below rates offered by most online banks and many competing credit unions
Website provides minimal information about non-savings products and services
Limited detail about loan products, eligibility requirements, or other member services
Small local institution may have fewer branches and ATM locations compared to larger credit unions
best_for: Brownfield, TX residents seeking local financial services with community roots
Conservative savers prioritizing NCUA insurance protection over interest maximization
Members wanting to support a member-owned, non-profit financial institution
Customers requiring only basic savings accounts and online banking access
diagnosis: Brownfield Federal Credit Union is best suited for local Brownfield, TX residents who value community banking and federal deposit insurance over competitive interest rates. The primary caveat is that the 0.25% savings rate is significantly below market rates from online banks and competing credit unions, making this primarily appropriate for members prioritizing local service and membership stability over yield optimization.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
